structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: A routine 3-4% downside move or Binance-specific dislocation before noon ET could still flip this exact-minute contract to No.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: Personas mainly differ on how aggressively to discount exact-minute path risk versus the current 72k buffer.

prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

BTC being above $72,000 on the April 16 noon-ET Binance 1-minute close is still the base case, but the market looks somewhat overconfident because this is a narrow exact-minute, single-venue threshold test rather than a broad daily-close question. My post-synthesis view is that Yes is likely, but less secure than the market price implies.

## Why this may matter now

Market-implied probability is about 0.88-0.90 from the latest fetched Polymarket surface; my syndicated range is 0.82-0.88. That is still a high-probability Yes, but the edge versus market is marginal rather than clearly actionable. The only plausible mispricing is that the market may be slightly underweighting exact-minute and single-venue path risk.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

The swarm-implied center was effectively around the mid-0.8s, with most lanes at 0.86 except the base-rate haircut to 0.68 and risk-manager at 0.79. My final range stays close to that center but trims the upper bound below a full market-agreeing view because the fresh verification did not eliminate exact-minute path risk. I moved materially above the base-rate lane because fresh market data and the now-lower live market …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market's ~90% Yes price is broadly efficient because BTCUSDT is already around 74.7k on Binance with limited time remaining, though single-minute and single-venue settlement risk justify a slightly lower estimate.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of about 90% because this is a straightforward, active crypto threshold market.
Verify the exact contract mechanics and governing source of truth on the Polymarket rules page.
Check Binance documentation and live BTCUSDT data to confirm the settlement surface family and current distance above the threshold.
Conclude that Yes is still the base case, but trim somewhat below market because single-minute and single-venue settlement risk are real.
strongest_supports: Live Binance BTCUSDT checks during the run were around 74.7k, about 3.8% above the threshold.
Only about 43 hours remained until the relevant settlement minute.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move 3-4% in under two days, so the cushion is meaningful but not decisive.
The contract settles on a single Binance one-minute close, creating timing and venue fragility.
unresolved_ambiguities: Polymarket names the Binance website chart as the settlement surface, while this run used API checks as verification rather than literal settlement output.
No dedicated volatility model was run in this pass, so the estimate remains judgment-based rather than distribution-fitted.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A drop back toward 72k before settlement, or evidence of Binance-specific operational/chart anomalies, would materially reduce confidence in Yes.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the Apr 16, 2026 12:00 ET Binance BTC/USDT one-minute candle close, corresponding to 16:00 UTC during EDT.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality contract text plus direct Binance verification made the evidence floor adequate; independence is medium because the verification source is the same data family named by the contract.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than No because BTC/USDT is already above 72000 on Binance, but the market overstates certainty for a volatile exact-minute threshold test.
main_logical_chain: Contract resolves from one exact Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at April 16 noon ET, so exchange, pair, timezone, and strict threshold all matter.
Direct Binance data shows BTC/USDT is currently above 72000 by a few thousand dollars at the relevant comparison minute.
Recent same-time failures below 72000 show the threshold is active but not secure, so near-certainty is not justified.
That supports a Yes lean but at materially lower confidence than the 90%-91% market price.
strongest_supports: April 14 noon-ET-equivalent Binance 1-minute close was 75356.48, comfortably above the threshold.
Recent daily and noon-reference prints show BTC has entered a stronger low-to-mid 70k regime.
strongest_disconfirmers: The same noon-ET-equivalent reference point was below 72000 on April 12 and April 13.
A roughly 4.7% cushion is meaningful but not decisive over a 47-hour horizon for BTC.
unresolved_ambiguities: Rules name the Binance chart interface as source of truth, while verification used Binance API data from the same venue.
No independent secondary exchange-specific chart source was used beyond direct Binance data and the Polymarket rules surface.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would raise confidence if BTC stays above roughly 74k-75k through April 15 and lower it materially if Binance loses 72k and cannot reclaim it before the final 24 hours.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the April 16, 2026 12:00 PM ET candle, which corresponds to 16:00 UTC during daylight saving time.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good direct evidence for mechanics and current level, with low-to-medium source-of-truth ambiguity but only medium evidence independence.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: YES is still the base case because Binance BTC/USDT is already well above 72000, but the market is slightly overconfident because settlement depends on one exact noon ET one-minute close rather than a broad or daily price measure.
main_logical_chain: Check the contract mechanics and named source of truth.
Verify current Binance BTC/USDT pricing directly on the named exchange data surface.
Compare current price cushion versus the 72000 threshold and note that the market only needs one exact future minute close.
Discount the market slightly for exact-minute volatility and exchange-specific path risk rather than broad directional bearishness.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ticker check showed BTCUSDT around 74734.66 on April 14, materially above the 72000 strike.
Recent Binance daily and intraday candles show sustained trading above 72000.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact minute close two days ahead, so a sharp drawdown or Binance-specific dislocation could still flip the outcome.
Crypto can move several thousand dollars in short windows, shrinking the current cushion faster than a static spot view implies.
unresolved_ambiguities: No major source-of-truth ambiguity remained after verification, but future market structure stress could still matter.
The exact April 16 noon ET candle obviously cannot be verified yet; only current state and mechanics can be checked now.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move higher if BTC remains firmly above the mid-74k area into April 15/16 with stable Binance trading; I would move lower if BTC retests 72k, volatility regime worsens, or Binance-specific mechanics look less clean.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves at April 16 12:00 ET on a single Binance one-minute close, so short-window timing mechanics are central.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because the rules name Binance BTC/USDT directly; secondary contextual confirmation was adequate but not deeply independent.
